summary: Crystal‑clear soundboard capture with scorching versions of Scarlet Begonias, St. Stephen > N.F.A., The Wheel and a blazing Let It Grow.
review: The Bertha‑remastered SBD sounds spectacular – crisp highs, deep bass and virtually no hiss – despite occasional tape glitches noted by a few listeners. Musically the show shines: a tight Scarlet Begonias, an incendiary St. Stephen > N.F.A., a monster Let It Grow > Might As Well pairing, and what many call the best‑ever Wheel performance, all wrapped in a hot second set that keeps the energy rolling.

ai_rating.rationale: Average rating from 15 reviews is ~4.7 stars; the majority are glowing, detailed comments praise both performance and sound quality.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Very clear mix with superb bass definition; a few reviewers note minor tape cuts/glitches and one mentions slight bass distortion, but overall the remaster is described as the best‑sounding version of this show.

standout_songs: Let It Grow > Might As Well
show_quality.setlist_flow: Second set dominates with seamless segues – The Wheel driving into Sugar Magnolia, a tight Stephen > N.F.A. sandwich and a high‑energy Let It Grow pair – delivering an overall hot, forward‑moving show.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s tone is repeatedly praised (malty Travis Bean sound, “en fuego” on Let It Grow) though one reviewer notes he missed cues in the second set.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass generally lauded for depth; one comment mentions slight distortion, another extols the bass clarity of this mix.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith is highlighted for tasteful fills on Stella Blue and overall solid keyboard work.
band_member_comments.Drums: Generally solid; a reviewer blames a double‑time hi‑hat for throwing off cues, but most find the drumming tight.
